concepts.title: C*-algebras and examples
concepts.blurb: C*-algebras abstract norm-closed *-algebras of operators and keep spectral control.

concepts.title: Spectrum and functional calculus
concepts.blurb: The spectrum σ(a) is a non-empty compact subset of ℂ; the spectral-radius formula r(a)=lim‖aⁿ‖^{1/n} and a continuous functional calculus on normal elements make C*-algebras analytically tractable.

concepts.title: Commutative duality and spectra
concepts.blurb: Commutative C*-algebras correspond to compact spaces via Gelfand duality.

concepts.title: Positive elements and states
concepts.blurb: Positive elements a = b*b form a closed cone detected by σ(a) ⊆ [0,∞); positive linear functionals of norm 1 are states, generalizing Borel probability measures to noncommutative C*-algebras.

concepts.title: Von Neumann algebras and noncommutative geometry
concepts.blurb: Weak closures, states, and noncommutative spaces extend geometry to operator settings.
